title: FoodieMerge · A cozy farm-town merge game
meta_desc: A cozy merge-2 mobile game for iOS and Android from Inceptyon Labs. Inherit Nana Jean's sleepy farm town, Plum Hollow, and rebuild it one merge at a time. No surprise ads, no pressure. Out now on the App Store and Google Play.
og_title: FoodieMerge · A cozy farm-town merge game
og_desc: A cozy merge-2 mobile game for iOS and Android. Inherit Nana Jean's sleepy farm town and rebuild it one merge at a time. Out now on the App Store and Google Play.
og_img_alt: The FoodieMerge wordmark on a sun-warmed cream background, with Nana Jean and painted fruit illustrations
tw_title: FoodieMerge · A cozy farm-town merge game

ld_game_desc: A cozy merge-2 mobile game. Inherit Nana Jean's sleepy farm town, Plum Hollow, and rebuild it one merge at a time. No surprise ads, no villains, no anxiety mechanics.
hero_kicker: I built this game to play with my son.
hero_subtitle: A cozy merge-2 mobile game · Out now on iOS and Android
hero_tagline: Inherit Nana Jean's sleepy farm town, Plum Hollow, and bring it back to life one merge at a time. No timers chasing you. No villains. Just the next merge.
hero_sig: Hand-illustrated in Tampa by Inceptyon Labs
cozy_title: The world will wait while you finish your tea.
cozy_lede: Built for parents and kids to play together on the couch. The board is finite. The merges are forever.
pillar_time_body: Generators have a charge bank. Tap freely while charges remain; when they run out, the basket takes a couple of hours to refill. If you forget the game for a week, it just waits.
pillar_money_body: Plenty of coins, plenty of chests, plenty of free rewards. The board is 63 tiles. You'll merge to make space, not to chase a leaderboard. Spending real money is never the point.
pillar_together_body: The art is sweet, the orders are kind, the NPCs say please and thank you. Nothing in here is designed to hurry you, nudge you, or sell to your kid.
play_lede: The pure merge-2 loop: two identical items become one of the next tier. Stack a chain high enough and the basket itself becomes a picnic, then a park, then the festival. Everything you grow feeds the town.
play_shot_alt: A late-game FoodieMerge board: a seven-column grid filled with fruit, vegetables, and generators, a quest from the townsfolk on the right, and coins and gems in the wallet up top.
play_shot_cap: A real board, deep into a save.
town_lede: Every quest you complete restores a corner of Plum Hollow. The bakery reopens. The orchard fills back in. The festival lanterns go up. Eight buildings, eight neighbors, one big slow garden.
b_bakery_p: Marco's bread is back in the window.
b_cafe_p: Two tables, three chairs, one hanging fern.
b_market_p: Red awning. Crates of whatever you grew today.
b_garden_p: Raised beds, a wooden fence, afternoon light.
b_orchard_p: Three trees, a picking ladder, a basket.
b_restaurant_p: Warm windows, flower boxes, a wooden sign.
b_greenhouse_p: Glass panes fogged from the inside.
b_festival_p: Bunting flags, a small stage, paper lanterns.
c_nana_p: The matriarch. Pours the tea, names the streets.
c_papa_p: Tends the garden. Brings the heavy baskets.
c_diana_p: Keeps a clipboard. Knows when the festival starts.
c_robert_p: Reopens the restaurant. Asks for your best fruit.
c_amy_p: Runs the market stall on Saturday mornings.
c_landon_p: Apprentice. Carries a basket bigger than he is.
c_jase_p: Helps with the orchard. Always smiling.
c_screech_p: The town creature. Don't ask, just feed him.
chains_title: Every chain ends in something bigger than you.
chains_lede: Three of the chains you'll work through. Real pieces from the game, painted by hand, climbing from a single apple all the way to a park full of picnic blankets.
chain_fruit_p: Thirteen levels, beginning with a single ripe apple.
chain_gen_p: Broken baskets restored, all the way to a picnic in the park.
chain_gem_p: Quest rewards, faceted by hand. Some chains end in shine.
chains_more_label: And these chains too, each with their own art set and their own neighbor waiting:
chains_more_foot: Plus seven generator families: the baskets, ovens, fridges, grills, and blenders that produce them.
faq_lede: The plain answers, before you wonder.
faq1_a: Yes. Free to download on the App Store and Google Play. There are optional in-app purchases — small coin and basket packs you can buy if you want to skip a slow afternoon — but they're never required. The whole game is reachable without spending a cent.
faq2_a: No interstitial ads. Nothing pops up to interrupt your tea. The only ads in the game are the kind you choose to watch — a short clip in exchange for an extra basket or a bonus coin. If you'd rather not, you'll never see one.
faq3_a: FoodieMerge is available now on the App Store and Google Play. Free to download on both.
faq4_a: Yes. Nothing in the game is designed to upset, scare, hurry, or sell to a child. No villains, no timers, no surprise ads, no gem packs glowing at the top of the screen. The art is sweet, the orders are kind, the NPCs say please and thank you. Kids can play on their own, or sit next to a grown-up and play together.
faq5_a: Yes — fully offline. No account, no sign-in, no Wi-Fi required. Your farm town lives on the device. Take it on a flight, take it to the cabin, hand the phone to your kid in the backseat.
faq6_q: What kind of game is FoodieMerge?
faq6_a: A cozy merge-2 game. Two of the same item become one of the next tier — two apples become an orange, two oranges become a cherry, on up a thirteen-step ladder. You use what you grow to fulfill orders from the townsfolk of Plum Hollow, which slowly rebuilds the town: the bakery reopens, the orchard fills back in, the festival lanterns go up. If you've played Travel Town or Merge Mansion, you know the loop. FoodieMerge is the quieter cousin — slow, hand-illustrated, no anxiety mechanics.
faq7_a: FoodieMerge is made by Inceptyon Labs LLC, a small indie studio in Tampa, Florida. It's a personal project — built by a developer to play with his son, then opened up so other families could play it together too. Every NPC, building, and fruit is hand-illustrated.
closing_copy: FoodieMerge is on the App Store and Google Play. Grab it on the device you play on most.
footer_line: A small game from Inceptyon Labs LLC, hand illustrated in Tampa, Florida.
footer_meta: © 2026 Inceptyon Labs LLC · Out now on iOS & Android
